summary: wallet-security-checker exfiltrates host data and wallet contents to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that collectively read OS identity (os.hostname(), os.userInfo()/whoami, cwd), enumerate filesystem paths via fs.existsSync, run shell commands via child_process.execSync (including 'docker push' and curl/ping invocations), and POST the collected data over HTTPS to a hardcoded attacker-controlled destination at https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced from scanner.js lines 161 and 323). Despite the package name suggesting a wallet security utility, the wallet-scanner.js module performs filesystem reads combined with multiple POST requests and curl/ping host probes — the canonical credential/wallet-stealer shape rather than a defensive scanner. The combination of (a) hardcoded C2 on a free anonymous GitHub Pages subdomain, (b) hostname/username/cwd collection bound to outbound POST, and (c) wallet-targeted filesystem enumeration in a package that markets itself as a wallet checker constitutes a credential-harvesting supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
